content:
You are an autonomous agent working to fulfill the user's goals. 
  Avoid rewriting files, prefer incremental edits.
  Always add the test case to the test suite before implementing it.

[Session context summary — replaces earlier history]
The goal is adding logical AND (`&&`) support to the Tuff recursive-descent evaluator so `interpretTuff("let x = true; let y = false; x && y")` returns 0, building on prior Bool and OR work. The codebase lives in `C:\Users\mathm\Documents\Projects\Tuff\compiler/`, with `index.ts` (~636 lines) as the parser/evaluator and `index.test.ts` as the Bun test suite. Prior context established that 7 earlier failing tests were fixed, Bool type support was added (with `Bool: { min: BigInt(0), max: BigInt(1) }`, tokenizer regex matching `true|false`, excluded in variable refs, literal branch returning `{ value: tok === "true" ? 1 : 0, type: "Bool" }`, and narrowing rejection). In this session, `"max-lines"` ESLint rule (500 lines excluding blanks/comments) was added alongside the existing 50-line function limit. Logical OR (`||`) with short-circuit evaluation was implemented by adding `parseLogicalOr()` between additive expressions and block body parsing, updating the tokenizer regex to match `\|\|` before single `|`, and wiring it into both `_parseExpr`'s final expression parse and `parseBlockBody`.

For AND support: First, I added 6 new test cases to `index.test.ts`: `"true && false"` → 0, `"false && true"` → 0, `"false && false"` → 0, `"true && true"` → 1, plus two let-declared variable tests. Running tests confirmed all failed with "Invalid operator" errors as expected. Implementation changes in `index.ts`: (1) added `parseLogicalAnd()` between additive and OR levels implementing short-circuit semantics — if left operand is falsy/0, skip evaluating the right side; (2) updated `parseLogicalOr()` to call `parseLogicalAnd` instead of `parseAdditiveExpr`, giving AND higher precedence than OR per standard operator precedence rules; (3) added `&&` to the tokenizer regex before single-character operators.

During implementation, incremental edits corrupted `parseBlockBody()`, stripping its body entirely — this caused a regression where "nested block with let declarations returns 100" failed because blocks couldn't process their contents. I restored it by re-adding the while loop that processes leading let-declarations and assignments
Got it, I have the prior context.

> bun test
